name: Incident Triage — RAG + MCP + Vertex AI

nodes.name: MCP Get Failing Pods
nodes.notes: Calls a single MCP tool directly, run in parallel with MCP Get Events rather than sequentially behind one combined node — this is the parallel-MCP-calls change. MCP client node field names vary by n8n version; verify against your installed version on import.

nodes.name: MCP Get Events
nodes.notes: Runs concurrently with MCP Get Failing Pods and RAG Query — all three branches fire off Parse Alert at once instead of the agent calling tools one at a time. limit is bounded 1-100 server-side; see mock-mcp-server/server.py.

nodes.name: Merge
nodes.notes: 3 inputs: RAG Query result, failing-pods result, events result — all merged after running in parallel.

nodes.name: Analyze (Vertex AI)
nodes.notes: Attach a Vertex AI Chat Model credential per the article's Step 2. For local testing without GCP, swap for any chat model node you have credentials for (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama).

nodes.name: Log Execution
nodes.notes: Per-node latency timing: each Mark *Latency node stamps Date.now() as it passes through, so this node can compute how long each stage actually took — visible in the execution data even though n8n's canvas doesn't show runtime duration per node.
